UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED: (1) DEFENDANT PRESENTS EVIDENCE OF DUE DILIGENCE COMPLIANCE UNCONTROVERTED BY PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION SEE PARAGRAPH 25; (2) PLAINTIFF PRESENT NO EVIDENCE RE DUE DILIGENCE DISCOVERING FRAUD AND BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND PLAINTIFF ADMITS RECEIVING DOCUMENTS IN 2007 AND NO EVIDENCE OF ABILITY TO TENDER: (3) PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE LOAN IS UNDER MODIFICATION REVIEW. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-28-2013 Continued To Jul-31-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Philip T. Kilduff (415-673-0368) special appear for plaintiff Heather Daniels opposing party.
Atty. Tracy L. Anielski of Burnett LLP (858-566-1877) CourCall appearance for defendant Meridian Foreclosure Service moving party.

Discussion, the court adopted its tentative rulign as follows: DEFENDANT MERIDIAN FORECLOSURE SERVICE, FKA MTDS INC.,'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Meridian was required to investigate beneficiary/servicer's compliance declaration per CCC 2923.5. (Opposition 4:19-20). Plaintiff's opposition fails to address any possible amendments. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge A James Robertson, Clerk K. Liu, not reported.(D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-10-2013 Continued To May-14-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Cathy Hongola, Esq. (415-273-7470) for Deutsche Bank and Onewest Bank, defendants and moving parties / Brian Angelini, Esq. (310-686-1127) appearing through court call, for Heather Daniels, plaintiff and opposing party / Tracy Anielski, Esq. Robert Cohen, Esq. (800-492-2657) for NDEX West LLC, defendant

ARGUED - Court confirms no receipt of opposition to demurrer. Plaintiff's counsel informs the Court that opposition to demurrer was filed. Court instructs the plaintiff's counsel to email the copy of the opposition to the clerk immediately. Defendant Meridian Foreclosure Services' Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is continued to May 14, 2013 at 9:30am in Dept. 501. Court orders the plaintiff's counsel appearance in open court on May 14, 2013. Judge A. James Robertson II, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar May-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Cathy Hongola, Esq. (415-273-7470) for Deutsche Bank and Onewest Bank, defendants and moving parties / Brian Angelini, Esq. (310-686-1127) appearing through court call, for Heather Daniels, plaintiff and opposing party / Tracy Anielski, Esq. Robert Cohen, Esq. (800-492-2657) for NDEX West LLC, defendant

Plaintiff's counsel has no objection to the tentative ruling. Court grants plaintiff's request for twenty days for leave to amend. Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Deutsche Bank and Onewest Bank's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is GRANTED with leave to amend in twenty days to allege facts re: successor liability and statute of limitations. See Alfaro v. Community Housing 171 Cal.App.4th 1356. Plaintiff's and Defendant's counsel are ordered again to comply with SFLR 2.6B and 2.6C respectively in future. Order is signed in open court. Judge A. James Robertson II, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

DEFENDANT MERIDIAN FORECLOSURE SERVICE, FKA MTDS INC., DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Off calendar. The motion should be re-noticed for hearing in Department 501. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/PJB)

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian A. Angelini (310-686-1127) of Angel at Law, Inc. for Heather Daniels, plaintiff and moving party (Due to technical problem with CourtCall - plaintiff's counsel joined the line after case called and ruling issued) / Cathy A. Hongola (415-273-7470 ) of Allen Matkins for Onewest Bank and Deutsche Bank, defendants and opposing parties

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is DENIED: (1) defendant presents evidence of due diligence compliance uncontroverted by plaintiff's declaration see paragraph 25; (2) plaintiff present no evidence re due diligence discovering fraud and breach of fiduciary duty and plaintiff admits receiving documents in 2007 and no evidence of ability to tender: (3) plaintiff presents no evidence loan is under modification review. Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Smith, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cohen (800-492-2657) of Law Offices of Robert Cohen, specially appearing for Index West, defendant and moving party / Brian A. Angelini (310-686-1127) of Angel at Law, Inc. for Heather Daniels, plaintiff opposing party (Due to technical problem with CourtCall - plaintiff's counsel joined the line after case called and ruling issued)

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIOAL TRUST COMPANY, ONEWEST BANK, FSB (ERRONEOUSLY USED AS INDYMAC)'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is
DENIED as to 1st and 4th causes of action 2923.5 sufficiently alleged. GRANT with leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action fraud to allege facts re successor liability and statute of limitation, see Alfaro v. Community Housing 171 Cal.App 1356. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Smith, Not reported (D501)
